name: BBQ Pulled Pork
description: Slow-smoked pork shoulder pulled apart and tossed in tangy BBQ sauce, served on brioche buns.
instructions: Mix paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, cayenne, mustard powder, brown sugar, and salt into a rub.
Apply rub generously all over pork shoulder.
Place in a slow cooker with apple cider vinegar. Cook on low for 8-10 hours or high for 4-5 hours.
Shred pork with two forks. Discard excess liquid but keep some for moisture.
Mix shredded pork with BBQ sauce.
Pile onto brioche buns and top with coleslaw.
tips: The pork is done when it shreds effortlessly with a fork.
Reserve some unsauced pork — let people add their own level of sauce.

name: New England Clam Chowder
description: Creamy soup loaded with clams, potatoes, celery, and bacon in a thick cream base.
instructions: Cook diced bacon until crispy; set aside, keep the fat.
In bacon fat and butter, sauté diced onion, celery, and garlic until soft.
Add flour, stir 2 minutes. Gradually add clam juice and milk, stirring constantly.
Add cubed potatoes, bay leaf, and thyme. Simmer 15 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Add clams, cream, and cooked bacon. Heat through (do not boil).
Season with salt and white pepper. Serve in bread bowls with oyster crackers.
tips: Do not boil after adding cream — it can curdle.
The clam juice is where most of the flavor comes from — do not discard it.

name: Chicken Pot Pie
description: Creamy chicken and vegetable filling baked under a golden puff pastry crust.
instructions: Poach chicken in stock until cooked; shred. Reserve stock.
Dice potato, carrot, celery, and onion. Sauté in butter until softened.
Add flour, stir 1 minute. Gradually add reserved stock and milk until thick.
Add shredded chicken, peas, and thyme. Season with salt and pepper. Pour into a baking dish.
Top with puff pastry, tuck edges, brush with egg wash. Cut steam vents.
Bake at 200C for 25-30 minutes until pastry is puffed and deep golden.
tips: Cut vents in the pastry to let steam escape and keep it flaky.
The filling should be thick enough to hold its shape when served.

name: Baked Mac and Cheese
description: Creamy three-cheese macaroni baked with a crunchy breadcrumb topping.
instructions: Cook macaroni until just al dente; drain.
Melt butter, add flour, cook 1 minute. Gradually whisk in milk until smooth and thick.
Off heat, stir in cheddar, Gruyere, cream cheese, mustard powder, and salt until melted.
Fold in cooked macaroni. Pour into a buttered baking dish.
Top with panko mixed with melted butter and paprika.
Bake at 190C for 20 minutes until golden and bubbly.
tips: Three cheeses gives depth — sharp cheddar for flavor, Gruyere for nuttiness, cream cheese for creaminess.
Cook pasta slightly under al dente — it finishes cooking in the oven.

name: Buffalo Wings
description: Crispy baked chicken wings tossed in Frank's hot sauce and butter glaze with blue cheese dip.
instructions: Pat wings completely dry. Toss with baking powder, salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Arrange in a single layer on a wire rack over a baking sheet.
Bake at 220C for 45 minutes, flipping halfway, until crispy.
Melt butter, mix with hot sauce.
Toss baked wings in the buffalo sauce until coated.
Serve with celery, carrot sticks, and blue cheese dressing.
tips: Baking powder on dry wings creates crispiness rivaling deep-fried.
Do not sauce the wings until just before serving — the crust stays crispiest.

name: Jambalaya
description: Cajun one-pot rice dish with andouille sausage, chicken, shrimp, and the holy trinity of vegetables.
instructions: Brown sliced sausage and seasoned chicken in oil; set aside.
Sauté the holy trinity: diced onion, celery, and bell pepper with garlic.
Add Cajun seasoning, tomatoes, and bay leaf. Stir 2 minutes.
Add rice and stock. Return sausage and chicken. Bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce to low, cook 20 minutes without stirring.
Add shrimp in the last 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork, garnish with spring onion.
tips: Do not stir once the rice is in — let it develop a crust on the bottom.
Add shrimp at the end so they do not overcook.

name: Cobb Salad
description: Composed American salad with grilled chicken, bacon, avocado, hard-boiled egg, blue cheese, and tomato.
instructions: Chop romaine lettuce, spread on a large platter.
Cook bacon until crispy; crumble.
Slice chicken, halve eggs, dice avocado, halve tomatoes.
Arrange ingredients in neat rows across the lettuce: chicken, bacon, eggs, avocado, tomatoes, blue cheese.
Drizzle vinaigrette over the salad.
Garnish with chopped chives, serve immediately.
tips: A Cobb salad is traditionally composed in rows, not tossed.
The vinaigrette can be served on the side for a lighter option.

name: Po' Boy Sandwich
description: New Orleans sandwich of crispy fried shrimp on French bread with lettuce, tomato, and remoulade.
instructions: Peel and devein shrimp. Soak in buttermilk for 15 minutes.
Mix cornmeal, flour, and Cajun seasoning. Dredge shrimp in the mixture.
Deep fry at 180C for 2-3 minutes until golden and crispy; drain.
Make remoulade: mix mayo, hot sauce, pickle relish, and lemon juice.
Split bread, spread remoulade on both sides.
Layer with lettuce, tomato, and fried shrimp. Serve immediately.
tips: Cornmeal gives the shrimp that signature crispy, crunchy coating.
The bread should be crusty outside but soft and fluffy inside.

name: Cornbread
description: Southern-style golden cornbread baked in a cast iron skillet with buttermilk and honey.
instructions: Preheat oven to 220C with a cast iron skillet inside.
Mix cornmeal, flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
Whisk buttermilk, egg, honey, and melted butter. Combine with dry ingredients until just mixed.
Remove hot skillet, add remaining butter to sizzle, pour in batter.
Bake 18-22 minutes until golden and a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool 5 minutes in skillet, then slice and serve with butter and honey.
tips: Preheating the skillet with butter gives cornbread its signature crispy crust.
Do not over-mix — lumpy batter is fine.

name: Shrimp and Grits
description: Creamy stone-ground grits topped with sauteed shrimp, bacon, and a lemony butter sauce.
instructions: Cook grits in stock and milk, stirring frequently, for 20 minutes until thick and creamy. Stir in cheese and butter.
Cook diced bacon until crispy; set aside, keep the fat.
Season shrimp with Cajun seasoning. Sauté in bacon fat 2 minutes per side until pink.
Add garlic, cook 30 seconds. Add lemon juice and 2 tbsp butter; swirl to make a sauce.
Spoon creamy grits into bowls.
Top with shrimp and sauce, crumbled bacon, and sliced spring onion.
tips: Use stone-ground grits, not instant — the texture difference is dramatic.
The lemony butter sauce ties everything together.
